content.full_text:
[The image shows a heavily degraded, difficult-to-read typewritten document. While I can see there is text present, the quality is too poor to accurately transcribe the content with confidence. The document appears to be formatted in multiple columns with significant fading, creasing, and wear that makes individual words illegible throughout most of the page. Without being able to read the text clearly and accurately, I cannot provide a reliable verbatim transcription as requested.]

PEOPLE
FOR PROOF
THE CALIFORNIA COMMITTEE FOR NUCLEAR SAFEGUARDS

2315 WESTWOOD BLVD. LOS ANGELES, CA. 90064 (213) 474-3320

Request No. 75 i2c
Date Received 5-23-75
Response Due 6-11-75

Courtesy of the California Committee for Nuclear Safeguards
William M. Bennett
Director
State Board of Equalization

May 21, 1975

Chairman Dan Lowenstein
Fair Political Practices Commission
926 J Street, Ste. 522
Sacramento, Calif. 95614

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

We are in the process of forming a statewide
committee to support the Nuclear Safeguards Initiative
and would like to have a ruling on whether the
endorsement from other groups appearing on our letterhead constitutes acting in concert with our committee
(CC §2013). Obviously this would alter our plans
for forming a support group.

I would like to have your opinion in writing
as soon as possible.

Sincerely,

Jeanine Hull
Jeanine Hull, Director

embedding.qa_text: June 5, 1975 Ms. Jeanine Full, Director People for PROOF 2315 Westwood Boulevard Los Angeles, California 90064 Dear Ms. Full: Re: Request for Opinion NO. 75-126 Thank you for your letter of May 21, 1975 requesting an opinion with respect to the duties of People for PROOF under the Political Reform Act. Since your request raises no substantial question involving interpretation of the Political Reform Act, your request for a formal opinion is denied. The determination of whether two or more committees are acting in concert for purposes of the limitations on campaign expenditures is essentially one of fact. The listing of an organization on the letterhead of your committee would constitute some evidence of acting in concert, but would not necessarily be conclusive. Although no formal opinion will be issued in response to your request, the staff of the Commission is currently studying questions that may arise under the limitations provisions, and at least one hearing will be held within the next few months to consider such questions. The Commission will consider whether the issuance of regulations or guidelines will be helpful for organizations such as yours. If you have any further questions regarding this matter, I urge you to contact a member of the Commission staff. Sincerely, Daniel E. Lovenstein Chairman, for the Commission DHU:cjb May 21, 1975 Chairman Dan Lowenstein Fair Political Practices Commission 926 J Street, Ste. 522 Sacramento, Calif. 95814 Dear Mr. Lowenstein: We are in the process of forming a statewide committee to support the Nuclear Safeguards Initiative and would like to have a ruling on whether the endorsement from other groups appearing on our letterhead constitutes acting in concert with our committee (GC §82013). Obviously this would alter our plans for forming a support group. I would like to have your opinion in writing as soon as possible. Sincerely, Jeanine Hull Jeanine Hull, Director
